summary.scope: EPIC-1 (Core Task Platform) and EPIC-2 (AI Prioritization Engine) - all 6 stories across both epics

criticalPaths.path: Task Create → Validate → Persist → Sync → Notify
criticalPaths.description: Complete task creation flow including real-time sync to other clients

criticalPaths.path: AI Analyze → Score → Rank → Suggest → Apply
criticalPaths.description: AI prioritization pipeline from analysis to applied suggestion

criticalPaths.path: Filter Set → Query Build → Execute → Render
criticalPaths.description: Advanced filtering from user input to rendered results

tests.name: should reject task with empty title
tests.description: Validates that empty string title returns validation error

tests.name: should reject task with title exceeding 200 chars
tests.description: Boundary test for maximum title length

tests.name: should sanitize HTML in task description
tests.description: Security test ensuring XSS prevention in descriptions

tests.name: should accept task with all optional fields empty
should reconnect within 3 seconds after disconnect
tests.description: Tests automatic reconnection with exponential backoff

tests.name: should queue messages during disconnection
tests.description: Verifies message queue captures updates while offline

tests.name: should replay queued messages on reconnect
tests.description: Ensures all queued messages are delivered in order after reconnection

tests.name: should handle max reconnection attempts
tests.description: After 5 failed attempts, show permanent disconnect UI

testsCreated.targetFeature: AI service fallback and error handling

tests.name: should fall back to rule-based prioritization when AI unavailable
tests.description: Tests graceful degradation when AI service returns 503

tests.name: should cache AI results for 5 minutes
should timeout AI request after 3 seconds
tests.description: Ensures slow AI responses don't block the user

tests.name: should select multiple tasks and bulk delete
tests.description: E2E test for multi-select and bulk delete workflow

tests.name: should bulk change priority for selected tasks
tests.description: Tests bulk priority update via toolbar action

fixturesCreated.name: disconnectedWebSocket
fixturesCreated.purpose: Provides a WebSocket mock that simulates disconnection after N messages

fixturesCreated.name: aiServiceDown
fixturesCreated.purpose: MSW handler that returns 503 for all AI service endpoints

automationStrategy.approach: Risk-based test automation: prioritize tests for high-risk areas (real-time sync, AI fallback) and coverage gaps. Use test pyramid to determine appropriate test level for each scenario.
automationStrategy.prioritization: P0 gaps first (WebSocket reconnection, AI fallback), then P1 (validation edge cases, bulk operations), finally P2 (UI polish tests)

testLevels.rationale: Fast feedback on validation logic and data transformations
Verify service interactions, especially WebSocket and AI service
Critical user journeys only to keep E2E suite fast

patternsApplied.usage: All E2E tests use page objects for maintainability
All test data created via factories with traits
Clear separation in all test cases

recommendations.recommendation: Add Playwright visual comparison tests for TaskCard and TaskBoard components to catch CSS regressions
recommendations.impact: Catches visual regressions that functional tests miss

recommendations.recommendation: Implement Pact consumer-driven contract tests between frontend and API to catch breaking API changes early
recommendations.impact: Eliminates integration failures caught only in E2E tests

failureDetails.test: should timeout AI request after 3 seconds (ai-fallback.spec.ts)
failureDetails.error: Expected timeout at 3000ms but actual timeout was 3012ms due to event loop delay
failureDetails.resolution: Increased assertion tolerance from exact 3000ms to 3000-3100ms range

failureDetails.test: should replay queued messages on reconnect (websocket-reconnect.spec.ts)
failureDetails.error: Message order assertion failed - messages 4 and 5 swapped
failureDetails.resolution: Fixed message queue to use insertion-ordered map instead of object keys
